title: T-124 — Pending Invitations lifecycle backend list/resend/revoke
prompt:
# T-124 — Pending Invitations lifecycle backend list/resend/revoke

## Goal
Implement the backend lifecycle behind UX SET-010 Pending Invitations: list outstanding invitations, resend pending/expired invitations, and revoke pending invitations with permissions, org isolation, audit, and typed blockers.

This task supports T-119 UI and reuses the invitation creation contract from T-017. It is backend/API scope only; do not implement the screen here.

## Files in scope
- packages/auth/src/invitations.ts [create/modify]
- packages/auth/src/__tests__/invitations.test.ts [create/modify]
- packages/db/schema/invitations.ts [modify if missing lifecycle fields]
- packages/db/migrations/NNN-invitations-lifecycle.sql [create only if schema fields are missing]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given an authenticated admin with invitation view permission, when invitations are listed, then results are scoped to the current org and include email, role, invited_by, invited_at, expires_at, status, and action eligibility for Pending/Expired/Accepted states.
- Given a Pending or Expired invitation is resent, when seat-limit and role-assignment preflights pass, then a new single-use invite token/email is issued, expiry is refreshed, audit/outbox records are written, and the response distinguishes pending vs expired resend.
- Given a Pending invitation is revoked, when the actor has invite-management permission, then the invite is marked revoked/audited and cannot later be accepted; Accepted invitations are immutable and cannot be revoked through this lifecycle.
- Given cross-org ids, stale tokens, seat-limit failures, missing permissions, or invalid state transitions, when lifecycle actions run, then typed blockers are returned without partial mutation.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: add targeted tests for org-scoped list, resend Pending/Expired, revoke Pending, Accepted immutable, and typed blockers for permission/seat-limit/cross-org/stale-token cases.
- GREEN: implement lifecycle helpers/actions using existing auth, outbox, and audit primitives.
- Run targeted auth/db tests plus typecheck/lint for changed packages.

## Risk red lines
- Do not duplicate invite creation semantics outside T-017; lifecycle actions must reuse its seat-limit, TTL, role, and token contracts.
- Do not allow resend/revoke across org boundaries or without invitation-management permission.
- Do not revoke or mutate accepted users through invitation lifecycle code.
- Do not implement T-119 UI in this backend task.
## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED -> GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

acceptance_criteria: List invitations is org-scoped and returns SET-010 fields plus state-derived action eligibility for Pending, Expired and Accepted invitations.
Resend supports Pending and Expired invitations, refreshes expiry/token/email through existing invite contracts, and writes audit/outbox records.
Revoke supports Pending invitations only; Accepted invitations are immutable/read-only and cannot be revoked through this lifecycle.
Permission, seat-limit, stale-token, invalid-state and cross-org failures return typed blockers without partial mutation.
test_strategy: RED: targeted tests for org-scoped list, resend Pending/Expired, revoke Pending, Accepted immutable, and typed blockers.
GREEN: implement lifecycle helpers/actions against existing auth/db/audit/outbox primitives.
Run targeted auth/db tests plus typecheck/lint for changed packages.
